I don't look anyone in the eye because women take it as flirtation just for keeping eye contact and men take it as a challenge.  I'm so fed up and I can go on and on with everything on this list and so much more that I decided to isolate myself from the whole world and go out into the woods away from everyone and mind my own business and I'll be damned if moving into the middle of nowhere where there were no people wasn't far enough and I am now surrounded by people who for whatever reason want to invade my space and will not leave me alone. They offer assistance or things to me that I did not ask for and then get angry if I decline. I finally fenced off my property and put up no trespassing signs and even locked Gates and I'll be damned if they don't get out of their cars and try to walk over through or under the gates and walk hundreds of yards across my property to knock on my door. So no matter what I say or what I do or don't say or don't do it seems to make no difference. So now I'm taking the only approach I've ever seen that does make a difference. I'm not going to become so overly aggressive and so in their face and live up to every stereotype they want to throw at me that all they're going to want to do is get away from me and they're going to beg me not to even acknowledge them anymore. It's kind of like when you have an employee following you around a store while you're shopping and every time you turn around there they are and no matter what you do they won't stop following you until you finally stop what you're doing and you approach them and start asking them where things are and start making them show you and putting them to work and then suddenly they disappear. it's like the person at work who is constantly right there when the boss is watching but when the boss is not watching they're slacking off but as soon as the boss looks they're over there pretending that they're doing the same work you are and then when it comes time for recognition they jump right up and pretend that they were a part of it the whole time.  This is not my world and these are not my people and all they had to do was leave me alone.  Most messed up part is that even though I've done absolutely nothing to them I've had several do bad things or steal for me or just talk behind my back about me. I've asked all of them the exact same question and not one person has been able to answer it yet. "What did I do to you?" They can't answer it because I have never done anything to any of these people. They have a problem with me simply because of my existence and maybe it's for the reasons mentioned in this video. I really could care less why but the fact is that these people keep doing bad things to me and then pretend that I'm the problem. So I say if you're being treated this way embrace it and be a problem.

author: @rikumoritaka
text: 1. You don't seek validation: You are comfortable with being yourself and do not require external approval (1:12). 2. You are comfortable in silence: You don't feel the need to fill quiet moments, which others may find unsettling or unreadable (1:27). 3. People feel compelled to justify themselves: Because you are observant rather than judgmental, people often feel evaluated and start over-explaining their decisions to you (1:42). 4. You maintain steady eye contact: You don't treat eye contact as a power move, but your ability to stay present makes others feel exposed (1:56). 5. You say 'no' without over-explaining: Your clear, direct communication style can be rare and intimidating to those accustomed to soften their boundaries (2:07). 6. You make people feel exposed: Your high level of awareness often removes the 'mask' others are wearing, making them feel like their performance is being seen through (2:39). 7. You possess high sensory processing sensitivity: You process the world at a deep level and notice shifts in energy that others ignore, giving your presence a weight that is felt before it is understood (2:52).

author: @Aethelhald
text: Looking someone dead in the eyes and saying "no" and nothing more is like a superpower once you learn how to feel comfortable doing it. People aren't used to a simple, expressionless, robotic "No." so it catches them off guard. If they persist, an outright "You have no right to my time or attention, leave me alone." also works wonders. Again, nobody expects to get these types of responses. They don't know how to react to it. It's quite unnerving and it works.  Until my thirties I was too shy to do it. I'd end up getting roped in to all kinds of nonsense I didn't want to do because I was too shy to just say no. At a certain point though you've had enough lol.

author: @Fortis_etFidelis
text: A co-worker several years ago, confided to me I intimidated him and others for quite a while after I transferred to their work group. I laughed at the thought, he was dead serious. Asked him "why I would be intimidating to you?" He said because I was serious about our work but quiet, when I speak everyone listens. It made me feel good and bad. I apologized for intimidating him, I never intended to. He rejected the apology, saying never apologize for being the man and leader you are. It was very complimentary and stunned me a bit. I never thought of myself as the leader until that moment.  He has become a good friend and co-worker. Without realizing it, he opened my eyes to how I may affect people without intention, and am more conscious of it.  Intimidating others is not always a good thing, can be counter-productive at work, and explains why some people seem to avoid me.

author: @RandyGnome
text: Woo Hoo, I have non verbal psychological dominance. Years ago I was told I intimidate people. I asked how and nobody could answer, they all simply replied with you just scare the hell out of us. I never did anything mean or nasty to them. I didn't abuse them, I treated them like a normal person and was pleasant and polite. But apparently everyone was intimidated by me. This was in various jobs and in TAFE (technical school where you learn trades) and the students and teachers were intimidated by me. 30 years later I see this on YouTube and it fits me. I walk into a room and everyone stops what they are doing and look at me. Some say hi and others say nothing, then they go back to whatever they were doing. I get up and people around me flinch. People that get to know me lose their fear and start to act normally around me but most others don't. I was in hospital a month ago and the staff were intimidated by me too. One of the doctors was terrified of me and I said nothing to him. I didn't do anything to anyone but they all spoke very carefully around me. Just because I have a horn growing out of my skull, it doesn't make me evil.

text: When I was in my thirties a patron at the library where I worked told me that when I looked into her eyes it was as though I was staring into her soul. I will hold people accountable, including myself, if a boundary is crossed. Clients push all the time. One of my clients bought concert tickets in a neighboring large city. author: @GhibliForestASMR
text: The concept of Self-Containment (Sign 1) is the core of this entire phenomenon. author: @SuperGoatTV
text: I've had people say they thought I was "scary" until the spoke with me then they thought I was really nice. I had someone I had never met before but was part of a group outing open a straw dispenser as soon as my food arrived at the table without being prompted and I jokingly said, wow, what service! And she replied, you command it. As in my presence alone motivated her to do that, I always felt that was an odd thing to say at that time lol. Multiple times women said things like, how did you get through my walls? I spent years building and protecting myself behind my walls and you walked right through them like they didn't even exist, or something along those same lines. I have literally heard multiple women say I am terrified of dating you because you can see right through me and there's nowhere for me to hide. I have always felt like an outsider, I'm very likable but I often feel like people "run away" because with me they might see their real "self" in their reflection in my eyes, so maybe it's not me they are truly running from... Anyway... interesting video, it give me flashbacks into many moments of my life that fit this narrative perfectly lol Thank you.
